codeReview.summary: EncodeProgress component is well-implemented with all required features: Start Encode button, progress bar with indeterminate state for stream copy, Cancel and Retry buttons, status badges for all states (queued/converting/done/failed/cancelled), error display, and completion display with size comparison. 35 unit tests pass covering all component states and interactions.

issues.description: handleRetry useCallback has canRetry and onRetry in function body but doesn't include them in dependency array. This won't cause bugs in practice since the callback is recreated on each render when status changes, but violates React hooks best practice.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/services.yaml does not exist in the repo. No service definitions to verify against.
.factory/library/architecture.md does not exist. No architecture documentation to verify component boundaries against.

Worker documented a pre-existing build failure (ESM/CJS mismatch in src/main/ffmpeg-path.ts and src/main/ffprobe.ts) that was not addressed. This is correctly flagged as non_blocking since it's outside the feature scope.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues lists this as non_blocking with pre-existing severity

summary: EncodeProgress component implementation passes review. All feature requirements met: Start Encode initiates encoding via IPC, progress bar updates from worker messages, indeterminate state for stream copy (progress < 0), Cancel terminates encoding, status transitions fully implemented (queued→converting→done/failed/cancelled), completion display shows output path and size comparison. 35 unit tests pass. Worker followed frontend-worker skill procedure correctly. One minor code quality note: handleRetry callback has unused dependency array entries.
